params.text:
Welcome to your care role

Starting work in adult social care is the beginning of a career that genuinely changes lives. Every day, care workers help people live with dignity, safety and as much independence as possible. This short course introduces the foundations you will build on throughout your induction and, in particular, the early standards of the Care Certificate — the nationally recognised set of standards that new workers in England complete to show they can work safely and effectively.

By the end of this course you will be able to describe what adult social care is, understand your role and responsibilities, recognise the values expected of you, and know where care fits within a regulated sector.

What is adult social care?

Adult social care is the support given to adults who need help with everyday living because of age, illness, disability, mental ill-health or other circumstances. The aim is not simply to “do things for” people, but to enable them to live the life they choose — with control, choice and as much independence as possible.

Care and support happen in many different settings, including:

  • People’s own homes — domiciliary or “home care” visits, and live-in care
  • Residential care and nursing homes
  • Supported living and extra-care housing
  • Day services and community groups
  • Reablement and short-stay respite services

Whatever the setting, the person receiving support — not the routine or the organisation — is always at the centre.
